# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    @products = Product.all

    if params[:search]
      @products = filter_by_name(@products, params[:search][:name]) if params[:search][:name].present?
      @products = filter_by_category(@products, params[:search][:category]) if params[:search][:category].present?
      @products = filter_by_price_range(@products, params[:search][:min_price], params[:search][:max_price]) if params[:search][:min_price].present? && params[:search][:max_price].present?
    end
  end

  private

  def filter_by_name(products, name)
    products.where("name ILIKE ?", "%#{sanitize_sql_like(name)}%")
  end

  def filter_by_category(products, category)
    products.where(category: category)
  end

  def filter_by_price_range(products, min_price, max_price)
    products.where("price BETWEEN ? AND ?", min_price, max_price)
  end
end
